name: toolbar-3col-grid
description: Non-split toolbar (bt-toolbar) uses same 3-col grid as bt-toolbar__main: minmax(0,1fr) auto minmax(0,1fr). Left=actions, Center=connection+travel-indicator, Right=time-format+theme+settings. Mobile override reverts to display:flex.

name: toolbar-byteMetricsBadge-trailing
description: ToolbarPanel byteMetricsBadge prop: connectionBadge was split into a deprecated no-op prop (connectionBadge) and a new byteMetricsBadge prop rendered in bt-toolbar__main-trailing. Connection status badge removed entirely.

name: repl-panel-slot
description: BehaviourTreeDebugger accepts replPanel?: ReactNode slot; when set, a terminal toolbar button (bt-toolbar__repl-btn) appears that toggles replMode state, replacing the canvas area with the provided ReactNode. replMode and performanceMode are mutually exclusive — entering one clears the other.

name: repl-panel-prop-integration
description: replPanel prop integration: App.tsx passes ReplTerminal as replPanel prop to BehaviourTreeDebugger; use @ts-expect-error when the receiving package's types are not yet merged into the current branch

content: DEMO_PRIVATE_KEY in use-repl.ts must use seed 'bt-studio-demo-repl-key-v1------' (32 ASCII bytes) to match DEMO_SERVER_KEYPAIR in studio-plugins/src/repl-crypto.ts; any mismatch prevents out-of-box REPL
replTheme export: repl-theme.ts exports 'replTheme' (not ubuntuTheme) — Frida-style plain black (#0a0a0a bg), simple '> ' prompt, Menlo/Consolas font, no Ubuntu references

name: repl-tab-activity-guard
description: repl-tab-activity-guard: activity window overlay condition guards on !replMode (same as !performanceMode) — prevents overlay appearing on REPL tab; pattern: !performanceMode && !replMode && activityWindowEnabled && activityWindowVisible

name: toolbar-grid-auto-1fr-auto
description: Toolbar grid columns should be 'auto 1fr auto' not 'minmax(0,1fr) auto minmax(0,1fr)': actions column sizes to content (auto), center takes remaining space (1fr with min-width:0+overflow:hidden so travel indicator clips), trailing sizes to content (auto flex-shrink:0). Prevents TT chip expansion from compressing action buttons.

name: repl-e2e-test-structure
description: REPL e2e tests connect StudioAgent with ReplPlugin(serverPublicKey: DEMO_SERVER_KEYPAIR.publicKey), wait 200ms for handshake after isConnected, use trpc.repl.eval.mutate + trpc.repl.completions.mutate; tests fail pre-fix (plaintext broker) and pass post-fix (NaCl relay)

name: repl-ondata-vs-onkey
description: xterm.js term.onKey() fires on raw keydown BEFORE dead key/IME composition resolves; term.onData() fires after composition. For REPL terminals handling international keyboards, use onData for printable character input and onKey only for special keys (Enter, Backspace, arrows, Tab, Ctrl sequences). Filter onData: skip \r, \x7f, \x1b*, and chars < 0x20.

name: repl-keyboard-isolation
description: When BehaviourTreeDebugger has a global window keydown handler for shortcuts (Space/Arrow/Escape), it must early-return when replMode is active. Add replMode to the useEffect dependency array. Without this, time-travel shortcuts fire while the user types in the REPL terminal.

name: repl-cursor-tracking
description: cursorPosRef tracks cursor position in inputBufferRef; ArrowLeft writes \x1b[D, ArrowRight writes \x1b[C; redrawInputLine(term, text, cursorPos) positions cursor by writing \x1b[N D after prompt+text; Backspace and onData (mid-line insert) both use redrawInputLine; all operations keep cursorPos in sync

name: repl-ghost-textarea-css
description: xterm.js helper textarea may appear as ghost input at top of canvas if layout clips incorrectly; fix: inject <style> in JSX with .xterm-helper-textarea { opacity: 0 !important; left: -9999px !important; top: -9999px !important; }

name: repl-masked-privkey
description: KeyRow accepts masked prop; when masked && value.length > 4, displays value.slice(0,4)+'••••••'; title attr set to 'Private key (hidden)'; CopyButton still receives full unmasked value

name: repl-syntax-highlight-cursor-safe
description: highlightJs(text) wraps tokens in ANSI codes but returns same visible char count; redrawInputLine uses text.length (not highlighted.length) for cursor stepsBack so ArrowLeft/Right stay correct

name: repl-always-redraw-on-input
description: onData handler in ReplTerminal always calls redrawInputLine (never bare term.write(data)) so syntax highlighting stays consistent as token contexts change (e.g. typing a quote turns previous text into a string literal)

name: repl-arrow-redrawInputLine
description: ArrowLeft/Right handlers must call redrawInputLine() not term.write('\x1b[D'/'\x1b[C'). Direct escape writes caused visible arrow-char artifacts and unreliable cursor positioning. redrawInputLine fully redraws the input line, keeping xterm cursor in sync with cursorPosRef.

name: repl-ghost-textarea-css-in-head
description: xterm-helper-textarea CSS fix must be injected into document.head via createElement('style'), not as a JSX <style> element in the flex container. A <style> as a flex child can interfere with layout in some browsers. Inject in useEffect and remove in cleanup.

name: repl-autocomplete-navigation
description: hasSuggestions guard block must intercept ArrowUp/ArrowDown/Enter BEFORE history and eval handlers. Use redrawSuggestionsAndInput() (go up N+1 lines, erase, reprint list+prompt) when cycling; use clearSuggestionsAndRedraw() (go up N+1 lines, erase, reprint only prompt) on Escape/Enter-accept.

name: xterm-shadow-dom-style-filter
description: collectDebuggerStyles shadow DOM filter must include '.xterm' so xterm.css rules are copied into the shadow DOM; without it, the terminal renders unstyled inside BehaviourTreeDebugger

name: repl-writeln-eol
description:
repl-writeln-eol: writeln() normalizes embedded 
 to 
 via text.replace(/?
/g, '
') before appending 
. Fixes staircase indentation in multi-line REPL output (e.g. JSON.stringify results). Applied in packages/studio-ui/src/repl/ReplTerminal.tsx

name: repl-multiline-cursor-col-fix
description: repl-multiline-cursor-col-fix: cursor repositioning after multi-line redraw. After writing PROMPT+content and going up linesBelow rows, colNow=lastLineLen (or PROMPT_VISIBLE_LEN+lastLineLen for single-line). targetCol=(cursorLine===0 ? PROMPT_VISIBLE_LEN : 0)+charsBeforeCursorOnLine. movementLeft=colNow-targetCol; use \x1b[D/>0 or \x1b[C/<0. Naive 'go left by chars-after-cursor' over-moves on line 0 by PROMPT_VISIBLE_LEN.

name: repl-linesRenderedRef-reset
description: repl-linesRenderedRef-reset: linesRenderedRef must be reset to 1 on: Enter submit, Ctrl+C, clearSuggestionsAndRedraw, redrawSuggestionsAndInput. After term.clear() (Ctrl+L) also reset to 1 before calling redraw. Forgetting to reset causes next redrawInputLine to go up stale number of lines into previous output.

name: repl-local-echo-integration
description: xterm-readline integration: (1) loadAddon then override attachCustomKeyEventHandler for Tab (return false + async handleTab) and Shift+Enter (term.input('\x1b\r', true)). (2) Async read loop: for(;;) { line = await rl.read(PROMPT); await doEval(line); } catch re-prompts. (3) Tab completions via (rl as RlInternal).state?.buffer()/.update(text). (4) Multiple completions apply LCP to avoid layout corruption from writing above readline prompt.

title: xterm-readline over local-echo
rationale: local-echo uses term.on() (xterm v3/v4 API), incompatible with @xterm/xterm v5. xterm-readline@1.1.2 uses @xterm/xterm ^5.5.0 peer dep, has setHighlighter/setCheckHandler/setCtrlCHandler, built-in history with localStorage. Applied in behavior-tree-ist-8c46.

name: repl-alt-word-nav
description: Alt+Left/Right word navigation and Alt+Backspace word delete in ReplTerminal use wordBoundaryLeft/Right helpers with JS identifier char set ([a-zA-Z0-9_$]). Access rl.state.line.{buf,pos} (TS private, JS accessible via cast) to compute delta, then call state.moveCursorBack/Forward/editBackspace(delta). Return false from attachCustomKeyEventHandler to suppress browser default behavior.

name: repl-tab-stale-guard
description: Tab completion stale-guard: after await sendCompletions, re-read rlInternal.state and compare currentState.buffer() !== prefix; discard results if buffer changed during async gap. Also early-return on empty prefix (no point completing empty string). Use console.warn instead of silent catch for completion errors.

name: repl-tab-preventdefault
description: In xterm attachCustomKeyEventHandler, returning false only prevents xterm's internal key processing — the browser's native focus-navigation for Tab still fires. Must call ev.preventDefault() before returning false to keep focus in the xterm canvas and allow Tab autocompletion to work.

name: repl-highlight-char-fast-path
description: highlightChar(line,pos) should return false for word chars (a-zA-Z0-9_$) to use xterm-readline's single-write fast path. Returning true always triggers refresh() with multiple term.write() calls; xterm WriteBuffer._didUserInput processes only the first write immediately and defers the rest to setTimeout, causing visible flicker (cleared line briefly visible). Non-word chars (operators, punctuation, whitespace) return true to keep syntax colours current.

name: repl-tab-candidate-display
description: When Tab completions.length > 1 and LCP equals current prefix (no narrowing), print candidates on the line below the prompt via rl.write('\r\n' + candidates + '\r\n'), then rl.write('\x1b[2A\r') to move cursor back to prompt row, then call rlState.refresh() to redraw the prompt. The refresh() clearOldRows erases the original prompt line and rewrites it, leaving candidates visible above in scrollback. Mirrors bash/Frida double-tab UX.

name: completion-overlay-react-bridge
description: completion-overlay-react-bridge: Use useState+useRef bridge (completionStateRef/setCompletionStateRef) to expose React setState to a one-time useEffect terminal closure. Needed for HTML overlay from xterm's attachCustomKeyEventHandler.

name: repl-textarea-buttons
description: repl-textarea-buttons: Expose doEval from terminal useEffect via doEvalRef = useRef<((code: string) => Promise<void>) | null>(null), assigned inside the effect. Capture plain-text output in outputBufferRef<string[]>. Toggle popups with useState (inputOpen/outputOpen) + snapshot output on open. Popups use position:absolute bottom:100% inside a position:relative toolbar.

name: repl-client-class
description: ReplClient (studio-plugins/src/repl-client.ts) is the UI-side E2E REPL crypto class. constructor(privateKey) derives publicKey via nacl.box.keyPair.fromSecretKey. completeHandshake(agentHeaderToken) base64url-decodes the token, opens the NaCl box with the UI secret key, then derives c2s/s2c keys via HKDF. encryptEval/encryptCompletions use secretboxEncrypt+encodeEnvelope with s2c key. decryptEvalResponse/decryptCompletionsResponse use decodeEnvelope+secretboxDecrypt with c2s key. Exported from index.ts.

name: use-repl-auto-handshake
description: use-repl.ts auto-initiates E2E handshake via useEffect: polls trpc.repl.handshake.query({clientId,sessionId}) on 500ms retry until headerToken arrives, then calls openHandshake() and sets sessionKeys. sendEval/sendCompletions unwrap { encryptedPayload } from tRPC response (not raw string). Plaintext fallback removed: sendEval throws 'REPL session not ready' when sessionKeys null.

name: popup-click-outside-backdrop
description: popup-click-outside-backdrop: Modal popups use a position:fixed inset:0 backdrop div at lower zIndex to catch click-outside dismissals. popup div onKeyDown catches Esc for all children. Same pattern as CompletionOverlay.

name: repl-paste-input-highlight-overlay
description: Paste Input textarea uses HTML overlay pattern — position:relative wrapper, pre aria-hidden with position:absolute inset:0 showing highlightJsHtml() output, textarea on top with color:transparent caretColor:#e0e0e0. onScroll syncs pre.scrollTop/Left. Trailing newline in pre innerHTML ensures height parity.

name: repl-clear-command
description: doEval intercepts /^clear\(\)?$/ before sending to agent — calls term.clear() directly (in-closure). handshakeStatus distinguishes transient (trpc retry) from definitive (openHandshake decrypt) failure; only decrypt errors set {error:...} status.

content: highlightJsHtml() uses VS Code-style palette: keyword #c678dd, number #56b6c2, comment #686868, string #5af78e, regex #e5c07b, punct #e0e0e0. Distinct from ANSI replTheme colours used by highlightJs().

name: ws-event-type-narrowing-pre-t1
description: use-repl-monitor parallel protocol dep: when a monitor hook subscribes to a WS event type not yet in the UiInboundMessage union (T1 not merged), cast event as Record<string,unknown> before comparing event.t string. Avoids @typescript-eslint/no-unnecessary-condition false-positive on discriminated union narrowing. After T1 merges, replace cast with proper UiMessageType import.

name: repl-monitor-self-contained-ws
description: ReplTerminal calls useUiWebSocket() internally (not threaded from App.tsx) to get subscribe for useReplMonitor. Creates a second WS connection to the server but keeps ReplTerminal self-contained within its file scope. Functionally correct; the server sends each broadcast to all connected UI clients.

name: repl-monitor-self-eval-filter
description: repl-monitor-self-eval-filter: useRepl tracks sent encryptedPayload strings in a stable Set (sentEncryptedPayloadsRef.current). Monitor hook (useReplMonitor) accepts selfSentPayloads: Set<string> and skips repl.activity events whose encryptedRequest is in the set. This ensures the monitor only shows external (MCP, other clients) evals, not ones the local UI initiated.

content: RefTracesPanel latestStates memo: when viewedTickId !== null, filter events to tickId <= viewedTickId before computing last-known state per ref; otherwise time travel shows global latest instead of state at viewed tick

name: asProcedure-trpc-helper
description: asProcedure<TIn,TOut>(fn: unknown) helper in trpc.ts: wraps tRPC .query() method with explicit types to avoid TS2589 (deep type instantiation) from recursive schemas like TickRecord→SerializableValue. Pass fn as unknown so TS skips deep proc type evaluation. Needs eslint-disable @typescript-eslint/no-unnecessary-type-parameters.

content: RefTracesPanel formatEventValue(event): prefers event.displayValue (string) over formatRefValue(event.newValue); handles both wire formats transparently

RefTracesPanel grouped last-known states: getRefPrefix(name) returns first dot segment (null if no dot); getRefSuffix(name) returns post-dot suffix. groupedLatestStates memo builds groups: ungrouped (null prefix) first, then prefix groups sorted by insertion order. Group header uses bt-ref-traces__group-header class; grouped entries show suffix only.

RefTracesPanel grouped refs collapse/expand: collapsedGroups state (Set<string>) + toggleGroup useCallback; group-header is a <button> with disclosure triangle span (bt-ref-traces__group-toggle) + count badge (bt-ref-traces__group-count); children wrapped in bt-ref-traces__group-children div only when not collapsed.

RefTracesPanel adversarial QA patterns: disclosure toggle uses bt-ref-traces__group-toggle--expanded class; count badge .bt-ref-traces__group-count; ungrouped (null prefix) renders as first child with no .bt-ref-traces__group class; deeply nested dots (a.b.c) use first segment as prefix, remainder as suffix; collapse state (Set) persists across viewedTickId rerenders; stale dot uses --stale/--fresh classes; nodeId optional — state entry skips onFocusActorNode when absent
